content: When a refine/inventory ticket asks to compare N surfaces against a new analytical lens (role-card shape, loop-moves, anatomy gaps), scope the lens to the surfaces where it carries semantic meaning (e.g. actual LLM prompts) rather than running it across every deterministic-script/config row too — the latter produces mostly-null cells and quadruples the document for no signal.

Do not author full new role-card/persona content as a side effect of a size:S inventory ticket — an inventory ticket's job is to identify candidates (source prompts + one-line rationale) and defer authoring to a follow-up implementation issue, especially when the pattern being adopted (e.g. agency-agents role cards) has zero prior references in the repo.

When a refinement-prompt ticket is asked to consolidate a baked persona-prompt bundle (refinement-skills/ -> /opt/refinement-skills/) toward a clone-live Claude Skill layout, do not bundle the physical directory rename + reference updates (Dockerfile/entrypoint.sh/scheduler.sh/context_budget.py/etc.) with a same-ticket prose de-duplication pass, even if a sibling policy spec designed the target layout already. The rename is a baked-image-to-clone-live delivery-mechanism migration (different risk class, needs its own Docker/CI verification) and should stay its own follow-up ticket; only trim/merge prose in place at the current path.

When an Archon phase command (clone-live, DAG-dispatched, e.g. dark-factory-refine.md) and a baked reference-skill prompt (e.g. refinement-skills/orchestrator-prompt.md) describe the same procedure in duplicate prose, the Archon command is always the canonical owner and the baked file shrinks to a thin persona stub - not the reverse. The dispatched, clone-live file already carries the real operational detail (scope boundary, memory rules, model pins, publish templates); do not migrate that detail into the baked file even if it originated there. Keep the stub file present (not deleted) if any script enumerates it by filename for token-budget accounting (e.g. context_budget.py's _SKILL_PROMPT_FILES).

When a dynamic-context-injection ticket targets a phase command whose raw-dump gap is fed by an artifact produced in an UPSTREAM workflow DAG node (e.g. implement's continue-intent comments/pr_reviews/pr_inline_comments, replaced by the digest-comments node's comment-digest.md), do not also fold in wiring architecture_slice.py/context_pack.py in the same ticket — those two scripts are fully implemented but orphaned (no DAG node produces their output at all today), so wiring them requires adding a new content-injection DAG node in addition to a command-text change, a materially larger/riskier unit of work than a size:M text-only fix. Keep them as separate follow-up tickets.

When wiring a compact-artifact script's output into a phase command where the script already ran in a separate UPSTREAM DAG node (not invoked by the command itself, e.g. digest-comments before implement), fail-open on file presence/non-emptiness (`[ -s $ARTIFACTS_DIR/<artifact>.md ]`) rather than a script exit code — there is no exit code to observe from inside the command. Keep the original raw-read instructions as an explicit fallback branch rather than deleting them, since the producing DAG node can itself be conditionally skipped by a token_optimization feature flag, leaving the artifact absent on a run that still needs the raw fallback.
